path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020)\01. Halland - Dalarna.mp3
description: These remixers have done magic before (they did the Xenoblade soundtrack) and they've done it again by turning these two ambient tracks from this spin-off Minecraft game into an up-beat battle theme. THE Minecraft theme in smash no less. I have to say though I don't think it sounds that good in general, although the Dalarna section is kinda out there.
title: Halland / Dalarna
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 37: Minecraft

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020)\02. Earth.mp3
description: I didn't know this until today but this remix is from a mobile game called Minecraft Earth which was meant to be Pokemon Go but with Minecraft, except it totally failed because there was a fucking pandemic. The original track was an ambient piano piece and now it's this. It feels like a stretch for sure. Something about it being a remix of a song that nobody knows makes it feel generic. Or wait: it feels like music from a Christmas movie.
title: Earth
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 37: Minecraft

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020)\03. Clockwork Crafter.mp3
description: One of the Minecraft expansions had this battle music, incredible for a game without battle music! It plays when you fight against another player in a special kind of minigame on specifically the old console editions of the game, so it's not exactly well known. It's got a cool clockwork theme, not sure why. It's pretty good actually.
title: Clockwork Crafter
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 37: Minecraft

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020)\04. Toys on a Tear.mp3
description: Can't believe my main man Yuzo Koshiro made this. Normally he makes good music, but I guess he didn't have much to work with. Only this obscure track from the console version of Minecraft that is vaguely upbeat. Sakurai had to get his best composers to get anything started with this source material.
title: Toys on a Tear
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 37: Minecraft

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020)\05. Dance of the Blocks.mp3
description: Sakurai was like please Yoko, take this obscure track from the console version of Minecraft and do your thing with it, and she did. Maybe it would be better if Steve wasn't in smash, then Yoko could have spent the hours she spent on this doing a sick remix from Wario Land instead.
title: Dance of the Blocks
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 37: Minecraft

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020)\06. Glide.mp3
description: More legacy console editions represented. This one is alright I guess.
title: Glide
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 37: Minecraft

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 37 - Minecraft (Switch) (gamerip) (2020)\07. The Arch-Illager.mp3
description: For the purposes of this website I looked up the final boss of Minecraft Dungeons and wow it looks so shit. This is the overly epic music from that, remixed even. I don't think it's very good.
title: The Arch-Illager
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 37: Minecraft
